svg.aria-label: Piper Logo and Blueprint Title, Headers

g.title: Piper Logo and Blueprint Title, Headers

g.desc: Piperbot and Pip the Martian Mouse. Piperbot is a light-gray robot with a square head and rectangular limbs, walking forward on a diagonal rainbow path of yellow, green, and red stripes. Piperbot has a colorful rectangular logo on its chest and holds a large, pixelated tan wrench. Pip, a small brown mouse with large round eyes and a pink nose, sits perched playfully on Piperbot's right shoulder.
g.title: Pip and Piperbot - Decorative Graphic

g.desc: Several 10mm LEDs shown from a side profile. The LEDs feature a large, rounded 10mm diameter plastic lens. The set includes distinct colors—red, green, blue, yellow, and white—each with a slightly frosted, diffused casing. Two thin, silver metal leads of unequal length extend from the base.
g.title: LEDs - Electronic Components

g.desc: Two 12mm square tactile buttons shown from an elevated angle. Each button has a black plastic base with four metal pins for mounting. Snapped onto the top of each button is a round, colored plastic cap which creates a larger, comfortable pressing surface. The removable caps feature a hollow underside that fits securely onto the button's square plunger.
g.title: Pushbuttons - Electronic Components

g.desc: A resistor. The component consists of a small, dog-bone shaped cylindrical body, typically tan or light blue, with two thin, flexible silver wire leads extending horizontally from each end. The center of the body is slightly narrower than the ends. Around the middle of the cylinder are four or five vibrant colored rings or bands. These bands are the primary way to identify the component's value. The illustration shows a side profile, emphasizing the symmetrical, non-polarized design where both ends look identical.
g.title: Resistors - Electronic Components

g.desc: A small #4-40 3/8-inch brass screw shown from a side profile. The screw is made of a distinct yellow-gold colored brass metal. It features a pan head, which is a low-profile circular head with a flat underside and a slightly rounded top surface. The top of the head contains a Phillips-head cross slot for a screwdriver. The threaded shaft is 3/8 of an inch long and features fine, closely-spaced machine threads that run the entire length of the shank.
g.title: 3/8-inch #4-40 Screws (4) - Brass Metal
Traffic Light (1) - Electronic Module
g.desc: A narrow, rectangular "Traffic Light" circuit board. On the left side, three tiny, flat surface-mount LEDs are arranged in a horizontal row within a rectangular white outline. In the center of the board is a square tactile pushbutton with a small circular plunger that clicks when pressed. On the far right edge is a vertical 5-pin connector used for wiring. The board has rounded corners on the LED side and sharp, square corners on the connector side.

A USB-A to USB-C adapter. The adapter is a small, rectangular block with a metallic USB-C male plug (a thin, rounded oval) extending from one end. The other end consists of a USB-A female port (a large, rectangular opening) housed within the main body of the adapter. The drawing is shown at an isometric angle to display both the protruding plug and the internal socket.

text.desc: A USB-A to USB-C adapter. The adapter is a small, rectangular block with a metallic USB-C male plug (a thin, rounded oval) extending from one end. The other end consists of a USB-A female port (a large, rectangular opening) housed within the main body of the adapter. The drawing is shown at an isometric angle to display both the protruding plug and the internal socket.
text.title: USB-A to USB-CAdapter (1) - Computer Equipment

g.desc: A Micro USB to USB-A cable. The graphic shows a flexible cable coiled in the center, with two different connectors at the ends. One end is a standard USB-A male plug, which is a large, rectangular metallic connector. The other end is a Micro USB male plug, which is a much smaller, flat connector with a distinct trapezoidal shape (wider on one side and narrower on the other).
g.title: Micro USB to USB-A Cable (1) - Computer Equipment

text.desc: A Micro USB to USB-A cable. The graphic shows a flexible cable coiled in the center, with two different connectors at the ends. One end is a standard USB-A male plug, which is a large, rectangular metallic connector. The other end is a Micro USB male plug, which is a much smaller, flat connector with a distinct trapezoidal shape (wider on one side and narrower on the other).
text.title: Mirco-USB to USB-A Cable (1) - COmputer Equipment
text.tspan: Mirco USB to USB-A Cable x 1

g.desc: Grove to Male Jumper Cable, approximately 12 inches (30cm) in length. The cable consists of four thin, flexible wires bundled together. On one end is a 4-pin Grove connector, which is a white, rectangular plastic housing. On the other end, the four wires separate into individual male Dupont-style pins, which are thin metallic needles encased in small black rectangular plastic housings.
g.title: Grove to Male Cable (1) - Wires

g.desc: A 10-segment LED bar graph module. The component is a long, narrow black rectangular block. Embedded in the top surface are ten small, identical rectangular windows arranged in a perfectly straight horizontal row. Each window represents an individual LED segment that lights up independently. The module has a symmetrical, low-profile design with sharp right-angled corners.
g.title: Bar Graph LED (1) - Electronics Component

g.desc: A bundle of male-to-male jumper wires, approximately 15 to 20 cm long. The graphic shows five wires joined together side-by-side in a flat ribbon format. On both ends, each individual wire is terminated with a male Dupont-style pin—a thin, silver metallic needle encased in a small, black rectangular plastic housing. The illustration shows the wires trailing off the left side of the frame, but they are identical on both ends.
g.title: Jumper Wires (Multiple) - Wires
